title: DownloadTracker leaks memory when agent dies mid-transfer
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

## What is wrong

commit be34e67 removed the 15-minute stale-timeout pruning from DownloadTracker (dispatch.rs).
The removal was correct — the old time-based prune was overly aggressive and dropped in-progress
transfers — but it left no cleanup path for the case where an agent is killed or disconnects
while a file transfer is in flight.

## Where

teamserver/src/dispatch.rs — DownloadTracker struct (around line 1126).
The tracker is keyed by (agent_id, file_id). There is no code in agents.rs or the liveness
monitor that calls finish() or removes downloads belonging to a dead agent.

## Correct behaviour

When an agent row is removed or marked dead (e.g., via kill_agent or the liveness sweep), all
entries in DownloadTracker for that agent_id should be drained and discarded so that
accumulated Vec<u8> payloads do not persist for the lifetime of the server process.

The stale-timeout was an imperfect workaround for this; the proper fix is explicit cleanup in
the agent death / removal path.
close_reason: Cleaned up in-flight DownloadTracker entries when agents are marked dead or removed.

title: Implement process list panel
description: In crates/client: process list UI. Tabular view: PID, PPID, name, arch, user, session. Refresh button. Actions: kill process, inject into process (opens injection dialog), migrate. Filter/search by name or PID. Highlight current agent process.
close_reason: Implemented the client process list panel with refresh, filtering, process actions, and structured process list state.

title: HTTP listener injects configured response bodies into empty Demon replies
description:
Introduced by: Codex

teamserver/src/listeners.rs:794-799 builds callback_placeholder_response from the configured response_body, and teamserver/src/listeners.rs:2331 routes every empty parsed Demon response through that path. That means a listener Response.Body value is sent back on no-job polls and on callbacks that correctly produce an empty acknowledgement. The Havoc reference only applies response headers and writes the parsed agent response bytes (src/Havoc/teamserver/cmd/server/teamserver.go:278-280 and src/Havoc/teamserver/pkg/handlers/http.go:179-187).

Why it matters: as soon as an operator configures a cosmetic response body, agents start receiving non-protocol bytes where they expect an empty body or a Demon message stream. This is a silent interoperability break that current tests do not cover.

What the fix should do: reserve custom response bodies for fake 404 / decoy responses only, and keep successful Demon transports protocol-pure: empty body for empty replies, serialized Demon bytes for real tasking.
close_reason: Reserved configured HTTP response bodies for fake 404 decoys and kept empty successful Demon replies protocol-pure.

title: test: get_credential and get_loot single-item endpoints have zero test coverage
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/api.rs
**Gap type**: existence

`GET /credentials/{id}` (`get_credential`, line 1333) and `GET /loot/{id}` (`get_loot`, line 1515) are completely untested — neither the success path nor the 404 path has any test. The list endpoints (`credentials_endpoint_lists_filtered_records`, `loot_endpoint_lists_filtered_records`) exercise the collection route but never call the single-item route.

Any bug in how individual credential/loot records are fetched, serialized, or authorized would go undetected.

**Suggested test approach**:
Insert a credential/loot record via the database, GET it by ID via the API, assert 200 with correct fields. Then GET a non-existent ID and assert 404 with the appropriate error code (`credential_not_found` / `loot_not_found`).
close_reason: Added single-item API tests for credential and loot endpoints, including 404 error-code coverage.

title: DEMON_INIT registration persists agent state before CTR initialization is durable
description:
Introduced by: Codex / Cursor Agent

teamserver/src/demon.rs:173-179 inserts a newly parsed agent into the registry/database and only then performs a second write to persist the initial CTR state via set_ctr_offset(). Those writes are not atomic.

If the process crashes or the second DB write fails between insert_with_listener() and set_ctr_offset(), the agent is durably registered with ctr_block_offset=0 even though the teamserver already consumed the DEMON_INIT ciphertext. After restart, reconnect acks and callback decryption will start from the wrong keystream position for that agent.

Fix: persist the initial CTR offset in the same operation that creates the agent row, or wrap DEMON_INIT registration in a transaction so agent metadata and transport state become durable together. Add a regression test that simulates a failure between the two writes and verifies no half-registered agent remains.
close_reason: Wrapped agent registration and initial CTR persistence in one transaction and added rollback regression coverage.

title: DownloadTracker has no aggregate cap or expiry for partial downloads
description:
Introduced by: Codex

teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:934
teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:936
teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:939
teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:940
teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:943
teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:965

Each individual download is capped, but the tracker accepts unlimited concurrent partial downloads and never expires abandoned state. A single agent can open many distinct file IDs, keep each below Teamserver.MaxDownloadBytes, and force unbounded memory growth by never sending a close. That leaves the teamserver vulnerable to memory exhaustion even after the per-download cap fix. The fix should add aggregate accounting plus stale-download garbage collection, with tests covering many concurrent incomplete transfers.
close_reason: Added aggregate download buffering limits and stale partial-download pruning with tracker tests.

title: Client - Havoc UI Python compatibility layer
description: Continue red-cell-c2-3h7 by adding compatibility shims for existing Havoc client-side Python modules that depend on Havoc UI helpers beyond the core runtime API. Scope should cover a havocui-style module surface, richer command invocation context, and any remaining script manager integration gaps discovered while porting real Havoc scripts.
close_reason: Added Havoc-compatible client Python shims: havoc/havocui modules, Event callbacks, Havoc-style RegisterCommand parsing, multi-word command matching, richer command context, script manager command visibility, and compatibility tests.

title: python: additional event callbacks — response, loot, listener-change
description: The Python event system (client/src/python.rs) only exposes on_new_session and on_agent_checkin. Add: on_command_response(agent_id, task_id, output) fires when any command output arrives, on_loot_captured(agent_id, loot) fires when a file/credential is logged, on_listener_changed(listener_id, action) fires on listener start/stop/edit. Follow the existing callback registration pattern.

title: Unknown-agent reconnect probe returns silent 200 with no warning or audit entry
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

teamserver/src/listeners.rs:1304-1313 — when a Demon packet arrives with
command_id=DEMON_INIT and an empty payload (reconnect probe) for an agent_id
that is not in the registry, the server silently returns an empty 200 response
with no log warning and no audit trail.

Impact (two angles):
1. Operational blindspot: an attacker who has observed the Demon magic value
   can enumerate whether a given agent_id is registered by sending probes and
   comparing response behaviour (empty 200 vs ACK payload).  There is no log
   entry to alert defenders.
2. Registered-agent probe also injects response body: the empty payload goes
   through callback_placeholder_response() which returns the configured
   response.body if one is set, so even unknown-agent probes receive a
   potentially content-rich 200 response.

Fix: add a tracing::warn! when an unknown agent sends a reconnect probe, return
a fake_404_response() instead of an empty 200, and emit a lightweight audit
event for security monitoring.
close_reason: Unknown reconnect probes now warn, return fake 404, and persist a lightweight audit entry.

title: Teamserver - Payload Builder
description: Rust-based payload generation system for producing Demon agent binaries (EXE, DLL, shellcode). Wraps the existing MinGW/NASM compilation pipeline, applies profile-based configuration (sleep, jitter, injection settings, C2 listener endpoints), and delivers output to operators.

title: Implement loot logging and credential capture
description: In crates/teamserver: loot capture and management. Automatically detect and store: credentials in command output (pattern matching), downloaded files (via CALLBACK_FILE), screenshots (via COMMAND_SCREENSHOT). Persist to ts_loot table with metadata (type, agent, operator, timestamp, content/path). Broadcast LootNew event to operators.
close_reason: Implemented loot persistence for credentials, downloads, and screenshots with operator broadcasts

title: DNS listeners from profile are silently ignored at startup
description:
Introduced by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

crates/teamserver/src/main.rs:260-266 — `profile_listener_names` collects HTTP, SMB, and External listener names but omits DNS listeners. Any DNS listener defined in the `.yaotl` profile is therefore never started by `start_profile_listeners`.

DNS support was added after this function was written and the DNS case was not back-filled.

Fix: add `names.extend(profile.listeners.dns.iter().map(|l| l.name.clone()));` and add a corresponding test case to `profile_listener_names_collect_all_listener_types`.
close_reason: Included DNS listeners in startup name collection and added regression coverage in the main.rs profile listener test.

title: Implement command console (per-agent interactive shell)
description: In crates/client: interactive command console panel per agent. Input field with command history (up/down arrow). Output area with scrollback (formatted text, color for errors vs output). Tab completion for Demon commands. Display metadata header (agent hostname/user/pid). Multiple tabs or split view for multiple agents. Timestamps on output lines.
close_reason: Implemented the client command console workspace with history, completions, and styled per-agent output.

title: test: payload_builder pack_config missing coverage for DNS listener and proxy-disabled HTTP
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/payload_builder.rs
**Gap type**: edge case

`pack_config` has listener-specific serialisation branches tested for HTTP-with-proxy and SMB, but two paths are not covered:

1. **DNS listener** — `ListenerConfig::Dns` hits a different serialisation path. The `build_payload_rejects_unsupported_listener_protocol` test confirms it is rejected at a higher level, but the underlying `pack_config` branch for DNS is never exercised (it may be unreachable, which itself is not documented or guarded).

2. **HTTP without a proxy** — The `pack_config_matches_expected_http_layout` test always includes proxy fields. The no-proxy branch (proxy = None) writes different trailing bytes. `pack_config_ends_after_listener_specific_fields` uses SMB for this case. HTTP + no-proxy is not directly tested, so a bug in the proxy-absent branch is invisible.

**Suggested test approach**:
Add a `pack_config` test using `ListenerConfig::Http` with `proxy: None` and verify the proxy section is absent (cursor is empty after the expected fields). Add a test confirming DNS listener returns an error from `pack_config` (or is correctly unreachable).
close_reason: Added pack_config coverage for DNS rejection and HTTP listeners without a proxy.

title: Embed Python runtime in client via PyO3
description: In crates/client: embed Python interpreter using PyO3. Load .py scripts from a configured scripts directory. Expose a client-side Python API: red_cell.register_command(name, callback), red_cell.on_agent_checkin(callback), red_cell.agent(id) returning an agent proxy object. Python runs in a dedicated thread with GIL. Error isolation (bad script doesn't crash client).
close_reason: Embedded a dedicated PyO3 client runtime with script loading, agent check-in callbacks, and agent proxy access.

title: Implement job queue and COMMAND_GET_JOB handler
description: In crates/teamserver: handle COMMAND_GET_JOB (command 1) - agent polling for work. Dequeue all pending jobs from the agent's job queue. Serialize jobs into the Demon binary response format (command ID + params per job). If no jobs, return empty response. Handle COMMAND_CHECKIN (100) heartbeat: update agent LastCallIn timestamp in registry and DB, broadcast AgentUpdate event to operators.
close_reason: Implemented queued job draining, Demon job responses, and checkin heartbeat updates

title: External listener transport remains a declared but unimplemented stub
description:
Introduced by: Codex

teamserver/src/listeners.rs:1555
teamserver/src/listeners.rs:1559
teamserver/src/listeners.rs:1562

External listeners are still exposed as a first-class transport in the shared config and operator surface, but every start path hard-fails with a not implemented error. That leaves the architecture and operator UX claiming support for a connector type that cannot do any work. The fix should either implement the external service-bridge runtime or remove or deprecate the transport from the supported surface until it exists.
close_reason: Rejected unsupported External listeners during profile validation and listener creation/sync; legacy persisted entries still fail safely on restore.

title: DNS upload assembler accepts untrusted chunk total from client, no upload session cap
description:
In listeners.rs, try_assemble_upload (line 1473) accepts a u16 total value directly from the client DNS query and uses it as the expected chunk count, storing it in DnsPendingUpload::total without validation. An attacker can:
1. Send an upload query with total=65535 and seq=0 for a fabricated agent_id. This creates a DnsPendingUpload entry that will never complete (65535 chunks required) and stays in the uploads map for 120 seconds (DNS_UPLOAD_TIMEOUT_SECS).
2. Repeat with thousands of distinct fake agent_ids without triggering the cleanup (retain is only called on the next incoming upload query). Between upload bursts, stale entries accumulate in the uploads Mutex<HashMap> without being pruned.
3. The total field is also not validated for consistency across chunks of the same agent_id — subsequent packets with a different total are silently ignored since the entry already exists, but adversarial early packets can set total to an unexpected value for a legitimate agent.

Fix: (a) Add a reasonable cap on total (e.g. 256 or 1024 chunks max), returning 'err' immediately if exceeded. (b) Add a periodic background cleanup task (e.g. tokio::spawn with interval) instead of relying on caller-triggered retain. (c) Add a max uploads map capacity check (e.g. 1000 concurrent sessions).

Location: crates/teamserver/src/listeners.rs, try_assemble_upload, lines 1473-1516 and handle_dns_packet line 1381.
close_reason: Rejected oversized and inconsistent DNS upload sessions, added periodic stale-session cleanup, and capped concurrent pending uploads.

title: Implement operator authentication
description: In crates/teamserver: operator login flow. Operators defined in HCL profile (username + plaintext password, hashed at load time with SHA3-256 to match Havoc). WebSocket handshake: client sends {User, Password_SHA3}. Server validates against profile. On success: issue session token (UUID or JWT), associate with WebSocket connection. Reject unknown users or wrong passwords. Write unit tests.
close_reason: Implemented SHA3-based operator login, session tokens, and WebSocket auth flow with tests

title: Implement token, injection, and shell commands
description: In crates/teamserver: handlers for COMMAND_TOKEN (steal/impersonate/revert), COMMAND_INJECT_DLL (inject DLL into remote process with shellcode), shell command execution, run (execute with output capture), and spawn (new process with token). Serialize jobs correctly per Demon binary protocol.
close_reason: Implemented COMMAND_INJECT_DLL and COMMAND_SPAWNDLL encoder+callback handlers, extended proc_subcommand for Modules/Grep/Memory, added parse_memory_protection and optional_u32 helpers. 13 new tests covering all new handlers and encoders.

title: API key lookup uses non-constant-time comparison — timing oracle
description: In crates/teamserver/src/api.rs:127, the authenticate() method looks up API keys via BTreeMap::get(presented_key). BTreeMap string comparisons are NOT constant-time: they short-circuit on the first differing byte. An attacker with high-resolution network timing (e.g. co-located on the same LAN) could potentially enumerate valid key prefixes character by character. Fix: hash the presented key with HMAC-SHA256 (keyed with a per-process secret) before inserting into the BTreeMap, and hash the presented key the same way before lookup. Alternatively, store a SHA-256 (or bcrypt/argon2) digest of each configured key value in the map and compare digests. The subtle crate's ConstantTimeEq is unsuitable here since map lookup itself branches early; the pre-hashing approach is the idiomatic solution for bearer token comparison.
close_reason: Hashed configured API keys with per-process HMAC-SHA256 digests before lookup and added auth regression tests

title: Implement Python script manager UI
description: In crates/client: script manager panel. List loaded .py scripts with status (loaded/error). Load/unload/reload buttons. Script output console (stdout/stderr from scripts). File picker to load a new script. Error display for import failures. Status indicator showing how many commands each script registered.
close_reason: Implemented the client Python script manager panel with runtime-backed load, unload, reload, status, and output handling.

title: test: kill_date > i64::MAX path in parse_init_agent has no coverage
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

teamserver/src/demon.rs — tests module

## What is missing

The only kill_date test vectors in parse_init_agent tests use 1_893_456_000, which is well
within the i64 range. There is no test for:
1. kill_date == 0 (no kill date)
2. kill_date == u64::MAX or any value > i64::MAX — the overflow path at line 319 is never
   exercised

## Why it matters

The overflow fallback `unwrap_or(i64::MAX)` (red-cell-c2-2njj) is untested, so any fix to
that line would go unvalidated. Tests in this area drive detection of protocol handling
regressions.

## Fix

Add a test case that builds a DEMON_INIT packet with kill_date = u64::MAX and asserts the
current behavior (i64::MAX stored) or the corrected behavior (reject the packet). Add a test
for kill_date == 0 asserting None is stored.
close_reason: Added DEMON_INIT kill_date tests for zero and u64::MAX edge cases.

title: SOCKS relay write_client_data failure silently dropped in socket callback handler
description:
Introduced by: Codex

crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:2955

In the DemonSocketCommand::Read branch:
  let _ = sockets.write_client_data(agent_id, socket_id, &data).await;

Errors from write_client_data (broken pipe, unknown socket ID, relay
task exited) are completely discarded.  The SOCKS client that sent the
request never receives the data and receives no error signal.  The
operator session has no visibility into the delivery failure.

This contrasts with the error handling directly above (lines 2942–2950)
which does broadcast an operator error event when the agent reports a read
failure.

Fix: log a warning (or broadcast an operator error event) when
write_client_data returns Err, consistent with the error path immediately
above it.
close_reason: Dispatcher now logs and broadcasts reverse-proxy relay delivery failures, with regression coverage.

title: test: resolve_logging_config whitespace-only RUST_LOG env var is silently ignored — not tested
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/logging.rs
**Gap type**: edge case

`resolve_logging_config` explicitly trims and filters the RUST_LOG env var:
```rust
env::var(EnvFilter::DEFAULT_ENV).ok().filter(|value| !value.trim().is_empty())
```
A whitespace-only value (e.g. `RUST_LOG=" "`) should be ignored and fall through to the profile or default. This edge case is not covered by any test.

Additionally, the no-debug / no-profile / no-env-override baseline case (should produce `filter_directive="info"` and `format=LogFormat::Json`) is never tested directly — it is only implied by tests that set other parameters. A regression changing this default would not be caught.

**Suggested test approach**:
In the `resolve_logging_config_with_override` tests, add: (1) a call with `rust_log_override=Some("  ".to_owned())` and assert it falls back to the profile/default level; (2) a call with `(None, false, None)` and assert `filter_directive=="info"` and `format==LogFormat::Json`.
close_reason: Added logging tests for whitespace-only overrides and the non-debug default config baseline.

title: Define shared error types and agent/listener structs
description: In crates/common: define the shared domain types used across teamserver and client. AgentInfo struct (all fields from TS_Agents DB schema: AgentID, Hostname, Username, DomainName, IPs, PID, ProcessName, Arch, Elevated, OSVersion, SleepDelay, Jitter, FirstCallIn, LastCallIn, etc). ListenerConfig enum (Http, Smb, External) with full config fields. OperatorInfo. Custom error types using thiserror.
close_reason: Implemented shared common domain types and thiserror-based validation errors

title: Implement session graph (node visualization)
description: In crates/client: egui node graph visualization of agent topology. Nodes represent agents, edges represent pivot relationships (SMB links). Drag-to-pan, scroll-to-zoom. Node color by status (active/dead). Click node to open agent console. Use egui_graphs crate or implement custom with egui Painter. Show teamserver as root node.
close_reason: Implemented an interactive egui session graph with pivot topology, pan/zoom controls, status colors, and console-opening node clicks.

title: Audit webhook delivery can stall operator/API actions indefinitely
description:
Introduced by: Codex <noreply@openai.com>

crates/teamserver/src/webhook.rs:31 constructs a default reqwest client with no request timeout, and crates/teamserver/src/audit.rs:300-313 awaits webhook delivery inline after persisting each audit record. The same synchronous path is used from API handlers in crates/teamserver/src/api.rs:2393-2414 and WebSocket/operator flows in crates/teamserver/src/websocket.rs:2311-2332.

If the configured Discord endpoint accepts the TCP/TLS connection but never responds, operator login, chat, listener actions, and API tasking can hang behind the webhook call even though the notifier is documented as best-effort. Correct behavior: outbound audit notifications should be bounded by a short timeout and/or dispatched off the request path so an unavailable webhook cannot block teamserver control-plane operations.
close_reason: Bound audit webhook delivery with a timeout and moved notification dispatch off the control-plane request path.

title: quality: AgentInfo name collision — two structs with same name in red_cell_common
description:
Introduced by: Michel Klomp (Claude Sonnet 4.6)

common/src/domain.rs:283 — persistence model, agent_id is u32
common/src/operator.rs:340 — WebSocket wire format, agent_id is hex String

Both structs are named AgentInfo in the same crate. Callers must import one as
DomainAgentInfo (see api.rs:21) to disambiguate. New code written against either type can
silently use the wrong one (e.g. passing the wire type to a DB function expecting the
domain type) and the compiler only catches this at use-sites, not at the import site.

Fix: rename one of these types. Suggestions:
  - domain.rs: AgentRecord (persistence layer)
  - operator.rs: AgentWireInfo or AgentNotification (WebSocket wire layer)

title: HTTP listener never captures real peer IP — external_ip always 0.0.0.0
description:
Introduced by: Codex

crates/teamserver/src/listeners.rs:887,900 — Both the HTTP and HTTPS listener variants call server.serve(router.into_make_service()) instead of router.into_make_service_with_connect_info::<SocketAddr>(). Because ConnectInfo is not injected into requests, the http_listener_handler has no access to the peer socket address.

crates/teamserver/src/listeners.rs:2040 — extract_external_ip() falls back to reading the x-real-ip header, which is fully agent-controlled (spoofable), then returns '0.0.0.0' if the header is absent. In practice, all HTTP-connected agents register with external_ip = '0.0.0.0' unless a proxy sets x-real-ip.

Contrast with: DNS listener correctly uses peer.ip().to_string() from recv_from(); the websocket server uses into_make_service_with_connect_info() and ConnectInfo<SocketAddr>.

Fix: switch to router.into_make_service_with_connect_info::<SocketAddr>() in spawn_http_listener_runtime, add ConnectInfo<SocketAddr> extractor to http_listener_handler, and use the socket address as the primary external IP source (with x-forwarded-for / x-real-ip as opt-in fallbacks via behind_redirector).
close_reason: Added HTTP listener regressions for peer IP capture and trusted redirector forwarding.

title: test: restore passing red_cell doctests under cargo test --workspace
description:
**File**: teamserver/src/lib.rs
**Coverage quality gap**:
- `cargo test --workspace` currently fails during `Doc-tests red_cell` because rustdoc cannot resolve the generated `pyo3` extern for `teamserver/src/plugins.rs` while testing the crate root.

**Why this matters**: a workspace-wide test run is currently red even though unit and integration tests pass. That makes doctest coverage effectively unavailable for the teamserver crate and can hide future crate-root documentation or API regressions.

**Minimum test scenarios needed**:
1. Happy path: make `cargo test --workspace` pass with `Doc-tests red_cell` enabled.
2. Error path: add a regression check or documented reproduction for the plugin-enabled rustdoc configuration that previously failed.
3. Edge case: confirm the fix works in a clean workspace without relying on stale build artifacts.

**Test location**: crate-root doctest path for `teamserver/src/lib.rs` plus any supporting workspace test configuration needed to keep doctests running in CI.

title: Implement pivot management (SMB links)
description: In crates/teamserver: manage agent pivot relationships. When an agent reports an SMB pivot link, store parent/child relationship in ts_links table and registry. Route jobs for child agents through parent agent's job queue (encapsulated in pivot packet format). Handle pivot chain teardown when parent agent dies.
close_reason: Implemented SMB pivot link persistence, routed child jobs through pivot packets, and added recursive link teardown on parent death.

title: Implement agent response handler (CALLBACK_*)
description: In crates/teamserver: handle all agent callback response types: CALLBACK_OUTPUT (command stdout/result), CALLBACK_ERROR (error message), CALLBACK_FILE (file download chunk with offset/total). Match response to original request ID. Format output and broadcast AgentResponse event to operators. Handle multi-chunk file downloads (reassemble). Persist output to DB for history.

title: Implement file system commands
description: In crates/teamserver: file system command handlers. Upload (send file to agent in chunks), Download (receive file from agent via CALLBACK_FILE, reassemble, persist to loot), directory listing, file delete, file copy, directory navigation. Track download progress and broadcast to operators.
close_reason: Implemented Havoc-compatible filesystem task encoding, upload chunk staging, and download callback persistence/broadcasting

title: DownloadTracker accumulates data without a total-size cap — OOM from rogue agent
description:
Introduced by: Codex

crates/teamserver/src/dispatch.rs:787–811 (DownloadTracker) and
lines ~1319–1365 (FileWrite handler)

DownloadTracker.append() unconditionally calls
data.extend_from_slice(chunk) with no check on total accumulated size.
Downloads complete only when the agent sends FileClose, not when
data.len() reaches expected_size.  expected_size is used only for
progress display.

An adversary who controls a Demon agent (e.g., blue-team capture, modified
implant) can:
  1. Send DemonCallback::File with expected_size = u64::MAX.
  2. Send repeated DemonCallback::FileWrite packets (each capped at 30 MiB
     by MAX_AGENT_REQUEST_BODY_LEN per request).
  3. Never send DemonCallback::FileClose.
  4. Over N requests the teamserver allocates N × 30 MiB → OOM.

Since each FileWrite arrives as a separate authenticated HTTP request, the
per-request body cap does not protect the per-download accumulation buffer.

Fix: add a configurable max_download_bytes cap in DownloadTracker.append()
and return an error / drop the download when exceeded.
close_reason: Implemented in existing HEAD by acfa632 (download cap) with coverage verified in current workspace

title: AES-CTR block offset lost on teamserver restart — reconnect probe sends ack at wrong counter
description:
Introduced by: Cursor (Ubuntu-HTB-cursor, working on red-2bm)

crates/teamserver/src/agents.rs:61,69 — AgentEntry.ctr_block_offset
crates/teamserver/src/agents.rs:110-133 — AgentRegistry::load

ctr_block_offset is initialized to Mutex::new(0) in AgentEntry::new(), which is called from AgentRegistry::load(). After a teamserver restart, all agents' CTR offsets reset to 0, but the agent processes have their counters at position N (accumulated across all prior exchanges).

Two failure modes:
1. Reconnect probe (empty DEMON_INIT): server sends build_init_ack encrypted at CTR=0 instead of the agent's expected CTR=N. Agent decrypts garbage as the ack.
2. Direct callbacks (no re-init): server tries to decrypt at CTR=0 while agent sends data encrypted at CTR=N. All decryption fails.

In the Havoc protocol agents normally re-send a full DEMON_INIT when reconnecting (which would reset CTR on both sides), but the reconnect probe path (empty DEMON_INIT body) does not trigger a full re-init and is therefore broken after a restart.

Fix: Either persist ctr_block_offset to the ts_agents table and reload on startup, or explicitly invalidate the CTR state on reconnect to force a full re-init.
close_reason: Added restart regression coverage for reconnect ack and post-restart callback parsing under restored Havoc stateless AES-CTR semantics.

title: Teamserver - Core Infrastructure
description: Core teamserver infrastructure: Axum HTTP server setup, SQLite database layer (sqlx), in-memory agent registry, and event broadcast system. This is the backbone that all other teamserver components build upon.
close_reason: Core infrastructure verified complete: Axum server setup, SQLite/sqlx persistence, agent registry, and event bus are implemented and passing workspace quality gates.
